resources.string: Build a concept quickly with reusable blocks, live preview, editable layers, and a portable JSON spec. No backend involved.
Expand a group, long-press an object, drag it onto the phone, and drop it where the screen should place it.
Drag blocks in from the palette. Drag existing blocks freely; long-press a block to show resize markers.
Tap a layer to edit it. Position and size are controlled directly on the phone preview.
Drag the selected block directly on the phone preview. Long-press it to show resize markers.
Choose a name for this design. It will be stored in the app private files on this device.
A saved project with this name already exists. Replace it?
Expand a group, long-press an object, and drop it on Live Preview.
Start from a template or a blank design, then drag UI Objects into Live Preview. Save/load projects locally or export JSON.
Tap a group to expand or collapse it. Long-press a UI Object and drag it into Live Preview.
Choose a real-life template, or tap Start new for a blank canvas. UI Objects are directly below this section.
Drop UI Objects anywhere on the canvas. Tap to inspect, drag to move, or long-press to show resize handles.
Tap a layer to select it. Delete removes the selected layer.
Tap a widget or layer to open Inspector. Edit text, size, spacing, alignment, opacity, style, and colors. Color fields show swatches and include Custom color.

1. Enter the project name at the top. This is the name used when saving the design.

2. Use Dark skin to switch the builder interface between light and dark styles.

3. In Templates, choose a real-life starting point or tap Start new for a blank design.

4. UI Objects are grouped by purpose. Tap a group header to expand or collapse it.

5. Long-press an object, drag it into Live Preview, and drop it anywhere on the screen canvas.

6. Objects on the canvas are drawn as real UI controls, including buttons, text fields, text areas, search bars, tabs, dropdowns, checkboxes, radio groups, switches, sliders, navigation bars, FABs, chips, lists, dialogs, snackbars, ratings, loaders, maps, charts, media players, QR codes, and layout containers.

7. Move an object by dragging it directly on the canvas. Placement is free, not locked to rows.

8. Resize an object by long-pressing it. Drag side handles to change width or height, or corner handles to resize both.

9. Tap an object to open Inspector. Use the >> button to close Inspector.

10. Inspector edits title, supporting text, width, height, text size, padding, corner radius, opacity, alignment, full-width mode, and emphasized style.

11. Background and accent color fields show color swatches. Choose an existing color or use Custom color for RGB sliders and #RRGGBB hex input.

12. Layers lets you select or delete objects from a list when the canvas is crowded.

13. Save stores the project locally on this device. Load opens saved projects. Export copies the design as JSON.

14. If something fails, send Downloads/uidesignerFailLog.txt.
No log file found yet. When the app writes logs, they appear in Downloads/%1$s.
Could not read the log file
